article.body_preview:
The Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry (DCCI) today urged the government to focus on ensuring uninterrupted and affordable energy for both local and export-oriented manufacturing units to further accelerate Bangladesh's economic recovery in 2023.

Also, emphasis should be placed on improving the ease of doing business and developing an infrastructural environment that is conducive for attracting local and foreign investment, according to a press release from the trade body.

In addition, the

llm_connections.evidence_quote: resulting in some negative impacts on the import of energy, industrial raw materials and supply chain for export-oriented industries.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly discusses country-level import activity—imports of energy and industrial raw materials—so it matches the aggregate imports feature scope.

verification.quote_best_match: resulting in some negative impacts on the import of energy, industrial raw materials and supply chain for export-oriented industries.
verification.judge_reasoning: The quoted text appears in the article nearly verbatim: it says the foreign currency shortage and taka devaluation have had negative impacts on the import of energy and industrial raw materials. This explicitly links the described economic problem to disruptions in imports at the country level, matching the claimed scope.
